meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Marketing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build marketing automation to overcome a lack of portfolio. An advanced-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' trap, build a live marketing automation engine. By leveraging Antigravity for orchestration and Claude Code for rapid development, you demonstrate high-level engineering skills. This creates a functional, real-world asset that proves your technical competency to recruiters and clients far better than a static project ever could.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank white screen of your personal website, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. You have the skills, but the professional void—the dreaded 'no portfolio'—is a wall between you and your first engineering role. The market doesn't care about tutorials; it cares about systems that generate value while the developer sleeps.

We are going to bridge that gap by building a sophisticated marketing automation engine. This isn't just a project; it's a living, breathing proof-of-work. By combining the architectural orchestration of Antigravity with the iterative coding power of Claude Code, you will build a production-grade tool that transforms your GitHub from a student sandbox into an engineering powerhouse.

steps.title: Architecting the Automation Pipeline
steps.description: Define the data flow between your lead source and your CRM using Antigravity's state machine to ensure idempotency and error handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an orchestration blueprint for a marketing automation pipeline that ingests webhooks from a landing page, sanitizes input, and routes leads to a CRM API with retry logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure with antigravity-sdk and environment validation'

steps.title: Configuring the Lead-Scoring Engine
steps.description: Implement a scoring algorithm that evaluates lead quality before triggering automated email sequences.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a logic node that evaluates incoming lead metadata against a scoring schema, assigning priority levels based on company size and tech stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement logic for lead-scoring-service.ts using antigravity patterns'

steps.title: Integrating LLM-Powered Personalization
steps.description: Use Claude Code to wrap an LLM call that dynamically generates personalized subject lines based on lead context.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a task definition for an LLM-based content generator that takes lead context and produces a hyper-personalized email body.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate anthropic-sdk and create the email-personalization-module'

steps.title: Building Resilient Observability
steps.description: Add logging and telemetry to your automated flows to demonstrate production-readiness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a telemetry wrapper that tracks execution time and success rates for every step in the automation pipeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'add prometheus-style logging metrics to the orchestration engine'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Package the service into a container and draft a README that focuses on the architectural decisions made.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-level architectural diagram description and a professional project README that highlights the automation flow and technical hurdles solved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate docker-compose.yml and write comprehensive-readme.md'

pro_tips: Always wrap your external API calls in circuit breakers to prevent cascading failures in your automation.
Use structured logging from the start; it makes debugging production issues 10x faster when you're managing live traffic.
Treat your automation logic as idempotent; it should be safe to run the same lead through the pipeline multiple times without duplication.
Document the 'Why' behind your architectural choices in your README, not just the 'What'—recruiters look for decision-making maturity.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your code that logs actions to the console instead of firing real emails; it's a lifesaver during development.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway, set up a domain, and link it prominently at the top of your resume and GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an engineer who builds functional, scalable systems. This project is the first brick in your professional foundation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
